title: tephrosia villosa subsp. ehrenbergiana
intro: Tephrosia villosa subsp. ehrenbergiana, commonly known as ehrenberg's tephrosia or weeping tephrosia, is a plant species belonging to the legume family. It is traditionally used in African medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's health benefits include treating fever, malaria, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a treatment for snake bites. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to create teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
